mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (0.11%) signals maximal monetary accommodation, compressing the equity risk premium and discount rates applied to future cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate remains low.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d return falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean forward return during sub-0.25% FEDFUNDS periods is not statistically greater than the unconditional mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines by more than 0.3 percentage points month-over-month from an elevated level, it signals an accelerating labor market recovery that boosts near-term consumer and investor sentiment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.3pp while UNRATE level remains above 6.0%
failure_condition: Triggered instances show forward 5d returns that are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero in more than 50% of occurrences across the walk-forward sample (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signals a broad-based consumer and residential investment rebound that neither series alone captures, and this joint demand impulse drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: The joint-trigger subsample fails to outperform the RSAFS-only or HOUST-only single-factor subsamples in mean forward 60d return, indicating no incremental interaction effect, or Rank IC of the joint signal is not significantly higher than either univariate signal.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-recovery expansion regime following a demand shock, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects re-engaged manufacturing capacity that leads earnings recoveries, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the same acceleration in a late-cycle regime does not carry the same signal because capacity is already near full utilization.
regime_hint: early recovery expansion after recession trough
failure_condition: In the designated early-recovery regime, INDPRO month-over-month acceleration fails to correlate positively with forward 20d returns (Rank IC < 0.03), or the effect is equally strong outside the specified regime, disconfirming regime-conditionality.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over consecutive months reflects durable labor income growth that supports consumption and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days), regardless of the prevailing macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth and forward 60d return is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or periods of rising PAYEMS show forward returns statistically indistinguishable from periods of falling PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses below the 0.25% zero-lower-bound threshold, markets price in an implicit central bank policy put that reduces near-term downside tail risk, producing an immediate positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean forward 5d return in periods immediately following the FEDFUNDS<=0.25% crossing is not significantly different from the unconditional 5d mean return (p>0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one distinct historical ZIRP episode.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth (GDPC1) confirmed jointly by rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates that headline GDP growth is genuinely consumption-driven rather than a statistical artifact or inventory effect, and this confirmed demand strength produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; GDP growth without corroborating retail sales strength does not reliably predict returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 3%
failure_condition: The joint GDPC1-RSAFS trigger subsample does not produce higher mean forward 20d returns than the GDPC1-only trigger subsample, indicating the interaction adds no predictive value beyond GDP alone.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) rebounds while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains persistently elevated above 6.5%, this divergence signals a jobless, capital-driven recovery that lacks broad-based labor income support, creating fragile equity gains prone to reversal, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days) in this specific labor-slack regime.
regime_hint: jobless recovery with persistent labor market slack
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns in the joint-triggered subsample are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the negative return effect is not statistically distinguishable from the effect of INDPRO growth alone without the UNRATE condition.
